name: Qingming (Tomb-Sweeping Day)
traditions: Sweeping and tending ancestral graves; offering food and incense; flying kites; planting willow branches; spring outings.
historicalBackground: Qingming has been observed for over 2,500 years, dating to the Zhou Dynasty. It combines ancestor veneration with celebration of spring's renewal.
description: China's Tomb-Sweeping Day, when families honor ancestors by tending graves and enjoying spring outings during the season of renewal.
